title: Lindernia spinifida
intro: Lindernia spinifida, also known as spiny-toothed Lindernia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae. The plant is valued for its traditional uses, which include its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Lindernia spinifida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lindernia spinifida include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin infections and wounds.
taxonomy: Lindernia spinifida is a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae. The plant belongs to the genus Lindernia, which is a group of aquatic plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lindernia spinifida is also known to be a part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes mint, lavender, and snapdragon. Within the order Lamiales, Lindernia spinifida is classified as a member of the clade Asterids, which is a group of flowering plants that includes plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers. The classification of Lindernia spinifida can be summarized as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Linderniaceae, genus Lindernia, species Lindernia spinifida.
